Steve Vinter

← Recipes

Granola

Ingredients

  • 4 cups rolled oats (not quick cook)
  • 1 cup sliced almonds, unsalted
  • 1 cup cashews, roasted and unsalted (we substitute pumpkin seeds and sunflower seeds)
  • 1 cup shredded coconut
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup maple syrup
  • ¼ cup canola oil
  • ¾ teaspoon kosher salt
  • 2 cups total dried fruit — raisins, golden raisins, dried cranberries, dried cherries, or any combination

Directions

  1. Preheat oven to 250°F and line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
  2. Combine rolled oats, almonds, cashews, coconut, and brown sugar in a large bowl.
  3. In a separate bowl, combine maple syrup, canola oil, and salt.
  4. Pour the maple syrup mixture over the dry ingredients and stir until well coated. Divide between the two prepared baking sheets.
  5. Bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes, tossing the granola every 15 minutes to prevent clumps. If your oven runs hot, the last 15 minutes may not be necessary — watch the coconut carefully to avoid burning.
  6. Remove from oven and let cool on pans for 30 minutes to an hour.
  7. Transfer to a large mixing bowl and toss with the dried fruit. Keeps up to a month in an airtight container.

Courtesy of The Chadwick Bed & Breakfast — the best B&B in Maine.
